title: Level 37.6
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 37.6:''' "True Eternity"'''}} {{Background|file=37.6 background.png}} {{Headerbox|file=37.6hovertext+diff.png|header="I am, in spirit, older than any human that's ever lived."
~A Wanderer's Note|speed=1%}} {{Class Custom Image|security= Unstable |entity= Extreme Psychological Hazard |safety= No Physical Threats|hexagon=Ψ|bg-image=37.6hovertext+diff.png|text-color=#6b4748|class=Ψ}}
'''Level 37.6''' is a sub-level between [[Level 37|The Poolrooms]] and [[Level 38|Air Pollution]].

==Description== {{Blurimage|file=37.6_Noclip.png|caption=The earliest discovered human-made image of '''Level 37.6'''.|width=350px}} As with most sub-levels, '''Level 37.6''', colloquially called simply the “Red Poolrooms”, is in many aspects similar to its mother level, [[Level 37]]. It too is a sprawling, architecturally nonsensical complex of interconnected rooms, halls, staircases, and atriums which are, to some degree, submerged in red-tinted water with completely submerged and dry areas to match. All of the level’s surfaces are entirely clad in pristine maroon-red tiles, which are taller and more diamond-shaped than the ones found in Level 37. Due to the level's temperature averaging around 46.2°C (115.2°F), a majority of the level's air is clouded by a thick red steam, which carries a sharp metallic smell and stings the eyes and lungs after prolonged exposure. The water's undulation is far more aggressive than that in Level 37. Its sounds are noticeably louder, drowning out most others. A substance nicknamed “Aeternol” is present in the level's waters. It is an immensely dangerous psychoactive drug acting as the primary threat of the level. Its presence has determined '''Level 37.6''' as uninhabitable and extremely dangerous to enter. It is recommended to avoid doing so at any cost. ==Aeternol==
'''
Description

''' In its pure form, '''Aeternol''' is a viscous deep red liquid most commonly found dispersed in the water of '''Level 37.6'''. '''Aeternol''' has a slightly lower evaporation point than water, making it a prominent part of the level's omnipresent steam. '''Aeternol''' has a strong metallic smell. Although an in-depth chemical analysis has never been conducted on it, it is likely that a notable part of Aeternol's composition is iron due to its red color and proven responsiveness to magnetic forces. Naturally, this has sparked rampant speculation regarding '''Aeternol''''s origins, leading to a plethora of theories regarding it. This has led to the creation of '''Aeternol''''s colloquial nickname "Godblood." Aside from being present in '''Level 37.6's''' water, '''Aeternol''' is also theorised to be the colouring agent responsible for the maroon-red appearance of the level's tiles. Unfortunately, this theory cannot be tested due to the level's tiles being indestructible, much like their counterparts in Level 37. '''
Ingestion

''' Upon entering '''Level 37.6''', the intake of '''Aeternol''' through inhalation, skin, or other means is, unless wearing protective equipment such as a hazmat suit, unavoidable. When ingested, '''Aeternol''' absorbs into the brain at an increasing efficiency, meaning that the rate at which '''Aeternol''' is absorbed increases with the amount already absorbed up to a certain maximum. It is theorised that this is caused by '''Aeternol''' having a potential weakening effect on the blood-brain barrier. {{Blurimage|file=37.6_watersample.png|caption=A sample of Aeternol water collected from '''Level 37.6'''.|width=350px}} '''
Effects

''' As mentioned, Aeternol is an extremely potent drug. Even a minuscule amount of it will have immense effects on the wanderer. Once absorbed, '''Aeternol''' will distort the functioning of the wanderer's prefrontal right cortex, the brain region responsible for time perception. As a result, Aeternol will progressively slow the speed at which the wanderer perceives the passage of time. This effect typically becomes apparent around 12 minutes after entering the level, when time perception is slowed down by roughly one half. At the 30-minute mark, the wanderer's perception of one second is slowed to three minutes. At this point, panic typically sets in, causing the intoxicated wanderer to move at higher speeds while searching for an exit. This often leads to slip and fall injuries or fainting due to heat and overexertion. This process continues up to the point where the wanderer's body absorbs approximately 167 micrograms of '''Aeternol''' per kilogram of bodyweight. At such doses, the wanderer may perceive 1 second as approximately 1 year. Additional '''Aeternol''' is excreted in urine past that point. The progression has been documented in this tablePlease note that this information has been calculated with an assumed trend after the 4-hour mark, as that is the extent of the available testimony on '''Aeternol's''' progression.:
{| class="fandom-table" |+ style="background-color:#2e0101"|
'''Table of Progression'''
!style="background-color:#2e0101"|Time Inside Level !style="background-color:#2e0101"|Absorbed Dosage (µg/kg of bodyweight) !style="background-color:#2e0101"|1 Second Equivalent !style="background-color:#2e0101"|Absorption (Approximate) |- |24 Hours |167 |1 Year |100% |- |20 Hours |115 |8 Months |95% |- |18 Hours |80 | 4.5 Months |85% |- |12 Hours | 56 |2 Weeks |75% |- |5 Hours |22.5 |6 Hours |50% |- |3 Hours |5 |20 Minutes |30% |- |45 Minutes |0.6 |12 Seconds |7% |}

'''Overdose'''

An '''Aeternol''' overdose is characterized as the point at which the wanderer's body has absorbed enough '''Aeternol''' to extend the perceived duration of 1 second into over 20 minutes. After this point, the rate of absorption becomes too high, and the wanderer's sanity is driven down beyond recovery unless treated almost immediately.
'''Treatment'''

Once ingested, '''Aeternol''' is metabolized and absorbed into the brain rather quickly, which makes treating the intoxication difficult. Only a few semi-effective treatment plans have been devised, each of them only working up to a certain point within the progression.
===Devilblight=== {{Blurimage|file=Devilblight.png|caption=Leaves in different stages of Devilblight infection.|width=350px}} '''Devilblight '''is a rare microorganism most commonly found on [[Level 630]]. As its name suggests, it is a fungal disease which affects a large range of flora. Once a plant is infected through a spore, '''Devilblight '''enters the veins of its leaves, where it begins reproducing rapidly. '''Devilblight '''has a distinct red color which is easy to spot on the leaves it infects. Although it spreads rather aggressively, it remains contained to a few areas in Level 630 due to the level's conditions. Notably, '''Devilblight '''contains an enzyme which is known to break down Aeternol, which makes its concentrate a central component of Aeternol intoxication treatments.
The general way to proceed when treating '''Aeternol '''intoxication is as follows: # '''Remove The Wanderer From The Area''' # '''Check Responsiveness''' - Typically, the wanderer is treatable as long as they actively responds to your speech. # '''Blood Filtration''' - This is best carried out through dialysis. # '''Administration of Devilblight Concentrate''' - It is important to carry out this step after you have already started blood filtration, as there are several dangerous toxins which are created as byproducts of Aeternol breaking down. # '''Increase Water Intake''' - Preferably [[Object 1|Almond Water]]. ==Unstable Regions== {{Blurimage|file=37.6_mall.png|caption=An adapted area resembling a shopping mall in an Unstable Region.|width=350px}} The level's stability varies from area to area. There are 18 known major hotspots of instability scattered throughout the level. Within these zones, '''Level 37.6''' and Level 37 seemingly overlap, causing the former to adapt sections of the latter in a modified state. Interestingly, the sections that '''Level 37.6''' adapts correspond to their original locations in Level 37 with shorter distances between them. This could indicate that space within '''Level 37.6''' is more compressed than the space in Level 37 in such a way where, for example, one kilometer in Level 37 may correspond to 500 meters in '''Level 37.6'''. These regions contain the most common entrances to and the safest exits from '''Level 37.6''', and it is thus recommended to exclusively search for an exit within them if possible. Due to their instability, the likelihood of noclipping within these regions is also increased. Noclipping certainly may serve as an exit from the level, but note that there is a high likelihood of noclipping to a level with a much more prevalant threat of physical harm than that of '''Level 37.6'''. (See '''Entrances and Exits'''.) It is recommended to do so as a last resort at your own risk. '''
The Towers

''' {{Blurimage|file=37.6_towers.png|caption='''The Towers''' above the roof.|width=350px}} While not always definite indicators, a common marker of unstable regions is an abundance of spiral staircases within them and their surrounding areas. When present, these staircases are consistently placed in a grid, continuing upwards seemingly infinitely. This phenomenon has become known as '''"The Towers."''' From their base level, '''The Towers''' continue upwards through multiple other areas and eventually through '''"The Roof"''', a featureless expansive plane clad in the same tiles as the rest of the level's surfaces. It is presumed to be the typical top of the level. The Roof is constantly damp with the same water present in the pools below it. The average temperature of The Roof is much colder, not allowing for the steam prevalent below it to form. Instead, the roof's air is clouded by a much thinner fog. Aeternol levels in this fog are greatly reduced, although it is still not safe to breathe. It is presumed that the space above The Roof, or perhaps the level as a whole, is enclosed within a larger object, as the fog would dissipate into [[The Void|the surrounding void]] without such precaution. The staircases continue upwards from here as well. Independent attempts have been made to reach some sort of top, although none have been successful. Today, the highest point to which any staircase belonging to '''The Towers''' has been climbed is at 16 kilometers upwards from its bottom-most stair.

'''Notice'''
Please refrain from sharing any of the multiple popular theories surrounding '''The Towers''' as none of them are confirmed. Concrete information regarding the phenomenon, its origins or properties is currently limited. Any confirmed findings made available to the M.E.G. will be publicized here.
==Entities== Entities residing in other levels have shown to instinctively avoid '''Level's 37.6''''s entrances, as entities are seemingly affected by Aeternol as well. This includes the [[Hydrolitis Plague]], which, once exposed to Aeternol, has been observed to significantly increase the speed of its reproductive cycle before stopping all of its biological activity entirely. No entities inhabit this level. ==Colonies and Outposts== Due to Aeternol's presence, the creation of any permanent settlements inside of the level has been deemed infeasible. However, a raider group that regularly enters the level based outside of it has been formed.
'''Descendants of Babel'''
* The group is based outside the level, primarily active around its entrances. * Knowledge regarding it is very limited. It is believed to be a cult based around the "Towers of Babel" theory.The "Towers of Babel" theory suggests that The Towers phenomenon was constructed by a civilization that once inhabited Level 37.6 to reach a divine entity, which, upon being reached, cursed the level by shedding its blood, Aeternol, upon it. * The cult is immensely dangerous, armed with bladed weapons and [[Object 29|Kalthoff rifles]]. The group reportedly abducts wanderers and subjects them to Aeternol intoxication as a form of religious sacrifice. * They possess multiple hazmat suits and regularly enter the level. * It has set up outposts around the most used permanent entrances to '''Level 37.6''', and it is thus recommended to avoid them and their immediate areas entirely.
==Attached Information==
'''Log In'''
The following information is accessible exclusively to members of the M.E.G.

'''Welcome, {{USERNAME}}.'''

[[File:37.6 meg.png|center|frameless]]
''' {{Collapse|num=2|initial=> Open "Research Overview"|final=v Close "Research Overview"|body=
The following is a brief overview of the research done on Level 37.6 in chronological order.
===Confirmation of Existence=== * Upon its existence being confirmed in 2013, a team of nine M.E.G. researchers from [[M.E.G.#Group_Structure|Research Team R-4]] was assigned to gather information and compile a report regarding the level. * The team began with collecting testimonies from wanderers who claimed to have information regarding the level. These revealed the existence of Aeternol, a few of its properties, and a general description of the level. ===Year 1=== {{Blurimage|file=37.6_Robot1.png|caption=An image captured with the RCRU.|width=350px}} * The team’s first in-level attempts at gathering information were carried out with the use of the Remote-Controlled Research Unit (RCRU), a video broadcasting remote-controlled rover commissioned from [[The_Kauer_Research_Organization|the K.R.O.]] equipped with a camera, microphone, light source, and a manipulator arm. * There have been 13 missions carried out via the RCRU within the level; one returning with a testimony from an unidentified wanderer before their death on their smartphone. The testimony was written on the device's "Notes" app and revealed more about Aeternol's progression. * This testimony confirmed the existence of Aeternol’s time perception-altering effects and qualified the level for Class Ψ designation. ===Year 2=== * Following an accident in which the RCRU was destroyed, researchers began hazmat suit missions utilizing convicts from various M.E.G.-related bases. * Four missions were carried out, resulting in the documentation of The Towers and The Roof. * In late 2015, a researcher was tasked by the project's overseer with sabotaging a hazmat suit in order to obtain direct testimony from a subject undergoing active Aeternol absorption. ===Year 3=== * The team erupted into conflict over the ethicality of the sabotage. This conflict escalated to a physical altercation, which resulted in the death of the saboteur. * The project was terminated. ===Year 7 - Present=== * A group named "The Redpool Rescue Society" was created by three of '''Level 37.6''''s former researchers and their associates in order to rescue wanderers within the early stages of Aeternol intoxication and perform assisted suicide on those beyond recovery. * Following expansion, the group currently consists of 7 groups of five situated in various levels near common entrances to '''Level 37.6'''. * The group's activity cositsts of: ** Preventing uninformed wanderers from entering the level. ** Monitoring activity via motion sensors and parabolic microphones placed in Unstable Regions and intersection-like areas. ** Regular expeditions for signal investigation and equipment maintenance. ** Research missions into the level. * While the RRS has been officially designated a raider group due to their euthanasia practice, the M.E.G. occasionally cooperates with them as they are the sole group currently conducting research on the level. Despite this, the M.E.G. engages in a disinformation campaign against them on orders from [ Official's Name Removed ], the research project's former overseer. It is supposedly to discourage the public from investigating the level, although his real motives are dubious. }}
{{Collapse|num=4|initial=> Open "Note #8"|final=v Close "Note #8"|body=
The following are screenshots of the last words of an intoxicated wanderer recorded on a smartphone.
Note #8
January 1, 1975 –00:00 AM I 1386 characters

It's the water. It makes the time go slower. In real time, I've probably only been here for a day. In that time, I have lived through millennia. Time started getting slower 10 minutes after I entered. First, a second lasted 10. Then it lasted an hour. Now it feels like a whole year. Back when a second was only a couple of weeks, I was running to find an exit. I looked through every possible crevice to find a ray of light uninfected by this putrid red hell. In my foolishness, I slipped and broke my foot. I've lived in pain ever since. Three thousand years of agony was enough.




□            [[Level List|◉]]             [[Level List|◀]]

Writing this far has taken me decades. A single letter takes me days to extend my thumb to. I have thought every thought a man's mind can think hundreds of times over. I've recalled my whole life and hallucinated living another. I've spoken to God and become him dozens of times. I have worked out the solution to every mathematical, physical, and socioeconomical problem in existence. I am, in spirit, older than any human that's ever lived. These will be my last words. I can't take it anymore. I will be drowning myself in the pools after writing this. Best case scenario, I'll breathe in immediately and suffer the sensation of the metallic chlorine water burning my lungs and my life fading away at a snail's pace for only 2 centuries. Please do not let anyone enter this purgatory ever again. Goodbye.
□            [[Level List|◉]]             [[Level List|◀]]
}}
{{Collapse|num=3|initial=> Open "The Towers of Babel"|final=v Close "The Towers of Babel"|body=
The following is a brief report by The Redpool Rescue Society regarding The Towers.


The Towers of Babel
Report by The Redpools Rescue Society


'''Overview'''
The Towers of Babel is the most popular theory regarding the “The Towers” phenomenon that occurs in low-stability areas of Level 37.6, as well as the most popular theory surrounding the level altogether. It speculates that Level 37.6 once served as Level 37’s counterpart to Level 0's [[Level 0#Additional_Information|Manila Room]], which would be evidenced by the level’s lack of the [[Phenomenon 15|isolating effect]] present in both Level 0 and Level 37. According to the theory, the level hosted a population of wanderers from ancient civilizations who would attempt to climb The Towers to reach an entity with supreme control over the level in a search for immortality. This would anger the entity, causing it to respond with shedding Aeternol, its blood, upon the level.

Recently, an exploratory expedition of ours has resulted in findings that we believe lend partial credibility to certain parts of this theory. This report seeks to inform the M.E.G. of these findings.

'''Recent Developments'''
On the 21st of July 2023, an expedition was conducted to Level 37.6. As a part of the expedition, researcher A. Quinn was tasked with climbing one of The Towers and attempting to reach the top. The researcher was experienced in these climbs and was thus the only one regularly performing them. Quinn reached the top of said Tower and made several discoveries. See the attachment below:

Attachment – A log of communications between Abraham Quinn and his assigned station in Level 11.
{{Collapse|num=45|initial=
> Open Attachment
|final=
v Close Attachment
|body=

Exploratory Expedition Log #31 – 7/21/2023


[00:00:00]

              11-2: Comms Check – AQ, come in.
              AQ: Reading you loud and clear, 11-2.
              11-2: Copy. Clear on your objective today?
              AQ: Yes, check air stats, attempt a tower climb and report any anomalies.
              11-2: Alright. Remember to run a full hazmat suit check before entering.              Out.

[00:05:23]

              AQ: 11-2, equipment checked. Ready to enter.
              11-2: Copy. Proceed. Out.

[00:10:47]

              AQ: 11-2, stats collected and dropped off at exit 1-2.
              11-2: Copy. Proceed to the climb. Out.

[01:03:22]

              AQ: 11-2, I have passed The Roof, continuing up.
              11-2: Copy. Stay safe, Abe. Out.

[04:11:07]

              11-2: AQ, status update?
              AQ: About nine kilometers up.
              11-2: Solid progress, AQ. Vitals?
              AQ: Heart rate elevated, enough oxygen. All nominal, 11-2.
              11-2: Good. Stay sharp. Out.

[07:22:13]

              11-2: AQ, report?
              AQ: Twenty kilometers. Vitals normal, oxygen declining but it should last              me around six more hours of climbing.
              11-2: Kilometer twenty? That’s four over the record. No anomalies at all?              Are you steady for 6 more hours?
              AQ: Affirmative on both. Out.

[12:09:54]

              AQ: 11-2, come in?
              11-2: Reading you.
              AQ: I... think I reached the top.
              11-2: What? Report vitals.
              AQ: I’m not hallucinating. They’re good. I can see a ceiling like 200 meters              up.
              11-2: Positive?
              AQ: Yes. Out.

[12:24:45]

              AQ: 11-2, urgent. Come in.
              11-2: Reading you.
              AQ: I’ve reached the top.
              11-2: What? Describe.
              AQ: Extensive flat ceiling of tile. Wet with condensation. The Towers don’t              connect; they stop just short. And, uh...
              AQ: There's writing across the ceiling. Unknown script. Seems ancient.
              11-2: Say again – writing?
              AQ: Affirmative. Documenting.
Quinn recorded the following:

[[File:37 6 animation,gif.gif]]

              11-2: No entities?
              AQ: No, no anomalies.
              11-2: Copy. Take air stats and begin descent immediately.
              AQ: Copy. Out.

End of Log


}}
Quinn climbed to the end of The Towers, reaching a plane nicknamed "The Upper Roof." On it, he discovered a supposedly "ancient" text later discovered to be written in Sumerian cuneiform. The text comprises 2 sections. The first roughly transcribes to “The deity is here. Observe our rise.” The second seems to be a list of 25 names.

'''Implications'''
The implications of these discoveries are immense. Firstly, the discovery of the ancient writing on The Upper Roof supports the Towers of Babel Theory, suggesting that early civilizations indeed inhabited Level 37.6 at some point in the past. Outside of this, the mere presence of presumably human-made text confirms that Aeternol was not a part of Level 37.6’s environment when it was created.

The first inscription, “The deity is here. Observe our rise,” implies that the original inhabitants were aware of the presence of some kind of god-like entity and viewed the climb as an act of ascension, which would match the story of The Tower of Babel. The second inscription, listing around 25 names, is speculated to represent the people who constructed or completed the climb of that particular tower.

Notably, Quinn stated that no entities were present at the top of The Towers. This could mean that the entity that would be responsible for the creation of Aeternol, should the theory be true, is either deceased or more centrally absorbed into Level 37.6. This could be evidenced by the fact that all available retellings of spiritual experiences from wanderers undergoing Aeternol intoxication involve speaking to a God in some way, although that could simply be a common delusion.

'''Conclusion'''
Further investigation efforts into Level 37.6 have been rather turbulent. Quinn has died of unrelated causes before he was able to complete any further climbs. We are currently attempting to train replacements who would be able to do so in his stead with no real progress. This is largely due to our limited resources. With this report, we hope to inspire further investigation into Level 37.6 from the M.E.G., which, despite our relations, we would be willing to assist in.
}}

}}}}
==Entrances and Exits==
'''
Entrances

''' Fortunately, entrances to '''Level 37.6''' are rare. Only a few have been found and documented. * Certain narrow hallways in [[Level 37]], [[Level 37.1]] and [[Level 188.8]] will eventually lead to an unstable region within '''Level 37.6'''. These hallways can be identified by an increasing red tint. * Noclipping within [[Level 37]] or [[Level 34]] has a chance of leading to '''Level 37.6'''. This entrance is the most dangerous, as it may place one far from any safe exit. * There are 6 documented permanent entrances through nondescript doors in various levels. Each one is guarded by members of the Descendants of Babel. They are as follows: ** 3 doors in [[Level 1]]. ** 2 doors in [[Level 11]]. ** 2 doors in flooded sections of [[Level 17]]. ** 1 door in [[Level 4]]. '''
Exits

''' * Noclipping within Unstable Regions may lead to either [[The Void]] or [[Level 293]]. * Noclipping outside of the Unstable Regions generally leads to [[The Void]]. * Exits frequently appear and disappear within Unstable Regions. They may lead to all of the levels in which doors to '''Level 37.6''' appear alongside [[Level 8]] and [[Level 65]].
'''Footnotes'''
